examples: Even a schoolboy's jape is supposed to have some ascertainable point.
Ascertainable effect is the essential content of a compromise contract.
Scattered matter, brought to an ascertainable density, closed, with the weight of the world.
If shares of stock used as compensation have an ascertainable value, they are immediately subject to income and payroll taxes.
In addition, rental must be fixed and known by the parties (or ascertainable by means of a formula) at the beginning of a contract.
